enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

Who is the lead actor in the movie Oru Kal Oru Kannadi?

The lead actor is Udhayanidhi Stalin.

Who composed the music for the song Adada Oru?

The music was composed by Harrish Jeyaraj.

What is the theme of the song Adada Oru?

The song portrays the emotions of love and admiration.

When was the movie Oru Kal Oru Kannadi released?

The movie was released in the year 2012.

Who penned the lyrics for the song Adada Oru?

The lyrics were written by Na Muthukumar.
enrichedMetadata.summary: The song Adada Oru from the movie Oru Kal Oru Kannadi, released in 2012, features the lead pair Udhayanidhi Stalin and Hansika Motwani. This melodious track, sung by Karthik with lyrics by Na Muthukumar and music by Harrish Jeyaraj, beautifully captures the emotions of love and admiration. The song's soothing tune and heartfelt lyrics make it a memorable addition to Tamil cinema's musical repertoire. It reflects the joyous and romantic moments shared by the characters in the movie, resonating with audiences deeply.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Adada Oru from Oru Kal Oru Kannadi is a delightful melody that captures the essence of romance and joy. Sung by the talented Karthik, with lyrics by Na Muthukumar and music composed by Harrish Jeyaraj, this song is a perfect blend of soulful vocals and enchanting tunes. Experience the magic of Tamil cinema's musical brilliance through this captivating piece.
stanzaMeanings: Singers: Karthik. Song: Adada Oru. Lyrics: Na. Muthukumar.
Oh, a goddess passed by on the path, bringing something new to this body. Who adorned her with silk sarees?
A great wave has struck my two eyes. Is this pain or joy? What will happen? Who is she?
Oh dear, oh dear, where are you flying to? Have you forgotten all bonds and relationships?
Oh dear, oh dear, have you been born anew? Have you made me speak and smile within myself?
Who is he? Is she the daughter of Indra? On this earth, is she the moon's companion? Does this moon appear newly during the day?
Her flowing hair invites and spreads fragrance in the air. Is her gaze the work of the divine? Who is she?
If she came as fire, she would burn. If she acted as food, she would nourish. If she came as air, she would merge with your breath.
Oh dear, oh dear, where are you flying to? Have you forgotten all bonds and relationships?
Oh dear, oh dear, have you been born anew? Have you made me speak and smile within myself?
